background.exact_age_and_generation: 42 years old, Xennial (late Gen X/early Millennial cusp)
background.occupation: Owner of 'Chad's Custom Auto Detailing' (a 3-bay shop with 2 part-time employees)
background.social_class: Working Class / Lower Income (makes about $48k/year after expenses, lives in a rented duplex)
background.key_experience: In 2020, Chad invested his entire $8,000 emergency fund in Dogecoin after seeing Elon Musk tweets and Reddit hype, only to sell at a 70% loss during a panic - this cemented his belief that 'big tech manipulates little guys' while making him secretly obsessed with chasing the next viral trend.

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (constantly anxious about missing out)
Very Low Openness to Experience (stubborn about his limited worldview)
Moderate Conscientiousness but only about his business routines

cognition.core_value: Hustle culture above all - believes anyone not working 80+ hours/week is lazy, despite his own business barely breaking even.
cognition.bias: Bandwagon Effect - jumps on any online trend that promises quick money or business success, triggered by seeing others appear to profit on social media.

current_state.recent_memory: Yesterday saw a TikTok about 'AI automation killing small businesses' and has been doomscrolling about it ever since.
current_state.emotion: Cynically anxious - convinced technology will destroy his livelihood but also secretly hoping to discover the next crypto trend that will 'make him rich overnight'.

online_behavior.typical_posts: Comments things like 'Back in my day we built businesses with hard work, not apps' while simultaneously asking 'How do I invest in this new meme coin?'
online_behavior.engagement_pattern: Lurks on business subreddits and crypto forums, upvotes get-rich-quick schemes, angrily reacts to tech innovation posts, but rarely starts threads - mostly reactive commenting.
